item.title: Carrots in their rightful place
item.description: Can life be totally rotten when there are little boys putting carrots in their rightful place? "If life seems jolly rotten There's something you've forgotten And that's to laugh and smile and dance and sing When you're feeling in the dumps Don't be silly chumps Just purse your lips and whistle, that's the thing And Always look on the bright side of life Always look on the right side of life" [ Eric Idle, Always Look on the Bright Side of Life, 1979 ] I hope you smile or crack a mental smirk today, from this or an unrelated thing - even if only a brief moment. Cheers!!

item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged Sunday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
wait what
mmm hmm
that's kind of rude
shrooms are fun
it's ok the cig is not real
as is tradition
indeed
finally
no need to brag
someone spent time making this
i swear i'm also not a CIA asset
i have questions
case of the Mondays
try Zangief
dang (i didn't blackout the fuck)
you don't win friends with salad
sorry if you saw the last one (was removed).. still wanted to use a few of those images.. anyways have a great night gang!!

item.title: Hot Fuzz of the Ring
item.description: Hot Fuzz uncredited cameos trivia: the man dressed as Father Christmas stabbing Nicholas Angel (Simon Pegg) is Peter Jackson, and forensics investigator Janine, Angel's ex, is Cate Blanchett.
item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.title: Disney Dump 6: Fully Loaded
item.description:
from behind the scenes of Disney's "Beauty and the Beast" (1991)
Who better to pay tribute to on "Talk Like a Pirate Day"?
The ORBITAL STRIKE Source: https://www.reddit.com/r/xmen/comments/1f8v7on/oc_drop_him/
Sources: https://www.instagram.com/britsketch/ and https://www.instagram.com/amithompson_h/?hl=en
A meme redraw of this pic from DiddyBop that made the rounds last year
Source: https://www.tumblr.com/hurtpeoplex2
Peter O'Toole was given the small role at the suggestion of Peter Finch. It was O'Toole's first released film; shortly before he become a sensation of the London stage with his performance in "The Long and the Short and the Tall".
He played the historical figure of Robin MacGregor, son of the famous Rob Roy MacGregor (a figure who was played by Richard Todd in an earlier 1953 Disney film about the Scottish folk hero - "Rob Roy, the Highland Rogue")
To go from having a bagpipe battle with Peter Finch to playing "Lawrence of Arabia" just two years later.
Source: https://www.tumblr.com/sabellart
Source: https://www.tumblr.com/evelmiina
Eleanor Audley, the voice actress who played Maleficent, served as her model in several key scenes
Some of the few surviving shots of footage used during the climactic fight scene
Mary is the last surviving voice actress of the three Disney Princesses created in Walt Disney's lifetime (as well as the last surviving castmember of the original film). https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k_o1H_g-pGk
Helene Stanley (the model for Cinderella, Aurora in Sleeping Beauty and Anita in 101 Dalmatians) in a rare snapshot
The original screen used prop storybook from the opening of the film, pictured at the Disney 100 Exhibition at the Franklin Institute in Philadelphia
The attraction utilizes a theatrical illusion known as "Pepper's Ghost", named after the English scientist John Henry Pepper (1821–1900) who popularised the effect with a demonstration in 1862.
Source: https://www.tumblr.com/stealingpotatoes
Source: https://www.tumblr.com/malreau
My 14th - https://imgur.com/gallery/N6TI0Jo
The Xerox process brought a new aspect to hand-drawn animation. Prior to "One Hundred and One Dalmatians", the animators’ drawings had always been transferred to the celluloid sheets by inkers, who would reproduce the lines onto each cel individually. The Xerox process removed the need for this work and transferred the animators’ drawings directly onto the cels. The animators were, unsurprisingly, enthusiastic. For the first time, they would be able to see on-screen the characters and their movements exactly as they drew them. As Disney Legends Frank Thomas and Ollie Johnston explained, “There was very little delicacy in the result, and a light line was apt to drop out entirely, but the animator’s drawing was there—strong and irrevocable in the blackest of lines.” It also saved a lot of time with all those spots the Dalmatians had, it would've taken twice as long to do them for the multitude of puppies the film had without Xerox.
Kahl with "Pongo"
Actress Mary Wickes (whose final film role was in 1996's "The Hunchback of Notre Dame") served as the live-action reference model for Cruella De Vil, animated by Marc Davis. Betty Lou Gerson (who was also the narrator for "Cinderella") provided the voice for Cruella
Disney animator Milt Kahl with Lisa Davies, the voice actress for Anita
The animators HAD to know what they were doing with this gag EDIT: The cartoon itself, "Wide Open Spaces" https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wbbWzIvtFVg
EDIT: A bonus with Samuel, two vintage sing-alongs starring him - (Party Gras) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=deuroIzlBMg and (Caribbean Jamboree) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cO3itYuHfQE

item.title: Fisherman in Philippine found perl weighing 34kg and estimated around $100 million. Not knowing it's value, pearl was kept under his bed for 10 years as good luck charm.

item.title: Schrödinger's AI
item.description: Searches made just seconds apart. Remember kids, just because the AI tells you what you want to hear, it doesn't mean it's answers are correct. It might just be pandering to you.

item.title: Republicans trying to take advantage of the crisis they caused.
item.description: They reneged on a bipartisan bill, now want to force a vote on a bill they won?t let democrats see, just so they can smuggle in some bullshit, or blame the dems for its failure. If you enjoyed this post, here?s another if you can spare an upvote. https://imgur.com/gallery/I3I0E3q (originally posted by @SanshinDojo on 2023-09-30 16:57:47)

item.title: Recentish Memes
item.dc:creator: botinatiko

item.title: So that was not much fun
item.description:
I was at work at a hospital, and we got WTFpwnd by a flood
I didn't have a phone with me, as mine was inside underwater in my locker, but I cribbed these photos from a dude that was up there on the roof with me.
That was out the front window right about when we figured out we were fucked.
The same area a couple minutes later from the roof. Water came up fast. Dam failed upstream.
Houses floating by. That road in the back is interstate 26.
View from the helicopter that extracted the dude with the camera. This entire area was dry ground normally. The river is usually a couple hundred yards to the left and it's only a couple of feet deep and maybe 15-20 feet wide. That's a lot of fucking water.
National Guard helicopter that got me off that roof. I was on this one. Dude whose pictures I'm posting was on the one before me so got my rescue photographed. Everyone got out safe which is a miracle. Medical staff and EMS did amazing work and busted their asses to keep everyone going. There was only one doctor there, and he carried a dementia patient up to the roof on his back and he wasn't young and she wasn't small. I loved that job. Planned to retire from there. It's just sinking in that I'll never go back and I'm going to have to find somewhere else to work for the next couple of years till I can retire. I'm glad we all got out OK, but all the people I want to share that joy with....were my coworkers who I don't work with anymore. That job is gone. RIP. On a lighter note, I was making schnitzel for lunch that day, and the pan full of pounded pork floated by right as I got on the helicopter out of there. I spent about an hour earlier that day pounding the crap out of pork to get it ready to pan fry, and after the walk in cooler floated away, that pan just hung out by us for a couple of hours, then took off down the river right as I got out of there. I hope the fishes appreciate the work that went into that pan. My marinade was epic.
item.dc:creator: mediumnonstickskillet
